name: Silverton Wind Farm
current_developer: PARF Company 8 Pty Ltd as trustee for the Silverton Project Trust
notable: 200MW, 58 x Siemens SWT-3.4-108 turbines. AGL PPA. AGL developed.

timeline.title: EPBC Referral 2009/4847
timeline.detail: Development of Silverton Wind Farm — Not Controlled Action

timeline.title: Financial close
timeline.detail: AGL reached financial close on the project

timeline.title: Construction commenced
timeline.detail: Construction began near Broken Hill NSW

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

eis_specs.document_title: Silverton Wind Farm Environmental Impact Statement (NSW SSD-5703 / EPBC 2013/7036)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: SWT-3.4-108 at 106 m hub height — tall tower for remote Far West NSW site
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Silverton, Far West NSW near Broken Hill — from on-site met mast
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling; P50 net yield after wake and availability losses
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Broken Hill 220 kV substation (TransGrid / Essential Energy interconnect), Far West NSW
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~20 km of 220 kV to Broken Hill 220 kV substation. Broken Hill is the terminus of the SA-NSW 220 kV interconnect via TransGrid.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: Connection to Broken Hill substation via TransGrid. Broken Hill is remote from main NSW grid, connected via 220 kV to SA. Silverton exports through SA route. Co-located with AGL 53 MW Broken Hill Solar Farm.
eis_specs.notes: 200 MW (58 x ~3.45 MW Siemens SWT-3.4-108), near Silverton, Far West NSW (near Broken Hill). Developer: AGL Energy. NSW SSD + EPBC approval 2013. Now operating (2019). Far West NSW — connected via 220 kV to Broken Hill and through to SA rather than main NSW grid.
